scenario: Server's resolved node URL does not percent-encode reserved characters in the ID path segment, producing two distinct URLs that decode to different IDs
rejected_because: Per gap A3 / PRD-106-R14, when substituting an ID into `node_url_template`, each path segment MUST be percent-encoded with the `pchar` rules of RFC 3986 §3.3, with `/` between ID segments preserved verbatim. Two URLs that decode to the same canonical ID MUST resolve to the same resource (PRD-106-R15).
violations: Literal space character left unencoded — invalid URL per RFC 3986.
`+` left unencoded inside a path segment — RFC 3986 reserves `+` for query interpretation in some contexts; safer to encode in path.

Server's request handler MUST decode percent-encoding consistently; if the producer URL omits encoding while the consumer's URL applies it, the two requests resolve differently — violating PRD-106-R15.
